tickerText: [TENNIS] Boss Open (Stuttgart): Ben Shelton defeats Taylor Fritz (6-4, 2-6, 6-4) for his first grass-court title — his third of 2026. HSBC Championships (Queen's): Donna Vekic wins women's singles over Emma Raducanu; Tommy Paul and Alex de Minaur advance as men's play continues • [BADMINTON] Australian Open (Sydney): Alwi Farhan defeats Dong Tianyao (21-13, 21-13) for his second Super 500 title; Akane Yamaguchi beats Pornpawee Chochuwong (22-20, 21-18) as China sweeps all three doubles golds • [SQUASH] PSA Squash Tour Finals (Paris, June 17-20): World champions Mostafa Asal and Amina Orfi top the draws at Centquatre-Paris; Marwan ElShorbagy and Tinne Gilis replace injured Youssef Ibrahim and Olivia Weaver • [PICKLEBALL] Boise PPA Challenger concludes in Idaho (June 12-14); PPA Asia 500 China Open begins June 17 in Beijing — Anna Leigh Waters named to TIME100 Sports List • [TABLE TENNIS] Gangneung World Masters Championships conclude (June 12); Kanak Jha climbs to world No. 24 — United States Smash opens June 26 in Los Angeles

newsItems.title: Shelton Claims First Grass Title at Stuttgart Boss Open
newsItems.excerpt: Ben Shelton defeated Taylor Fritz 6-4, 2-6, 6-4 in an all-American final to win the Boss Open in Stuttgart — his first career grass-court title and third of 2026. At Queen's Club, Donna Vekic captured the women's singles crown while the men's ATP 500 draw is underway.

newsItems.title: Farhan and Yamaguchi Crowned at Australian Open
newsItems.excerpt: Indonesia's Alwi Farhan swept China's Dong Tianyao 21-13, 21-13 to claim his second BWF Super 500 title of the year at the Sathio Group Australian Open. Akane Yamaguchi won women's singles over Pornpawee Chochuwong as China took all three doubles championships in Sydney.

newsItems.title: Asal and Orfi Lead Draws for PSA Tour Finals in Paris
newsItems.excerpt: World champions Mostafa Asal and Amina Orfi top the season-ending PSA Squash Tour Finals draws at Centquatre-Paris (June 17-20). Injury withdrawals opened spots for Marwan ElShorbagy and Tinne Gilis, with a record $710,000 prize pool on the line.

newsItems.title: Boise Challenger Wraps; PPA Asia 500 China Open Up Next
newsItems.excerpt: The Boise PPA Challenger concluded June 14 at The Flying Pickle in Idaho, with rising pros competing for ranking points. The tour shifts to Beijing for the PPA Asia 500 China Open starting June 17, as Anna Leigh Waters earns TIME100 Sports recognition.

newsItems.title: Gangneung Masters Conclude; Jha Climbs Rankings Ahead of US Smash
newsItems.excerpt: The XIOM 2026 Gangneung World Masters Table Tennis Championships wrapped June 12 after eight days in South Korea. Kanak Jha jumped six spots to world No. 24 following his Skopje result, with the United States Smash set for Los Angeles starting June 26.
